# Law corpus — provenance

Every file here was fetched from the **official Pakistan Code**
(https://www.pakistancode.gov.pk), published by the Ministry of Law and Justice,
by `backend/scripts/fetch_laws.py`. Statute text is a government edict — it is
the law, published by the state for citizens to read.

**No text in this directory was written, paraphrased, or generated by a model.**
Where a statute could not be fetched, the file was left as-is rather than filled
in. A documented gap is safe; an invented section number is not.

| File | Statute | Sections | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ppc.txt` | Pakistan Penal Code, 1860 (Act XLV) | 578 | Pakistan Code |
| `crpc.txt` | Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 (Act V) | 502 | Pakistan Code |
| `cpc_1908.txt` | Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (Act V) | 160 | Pakistan Code |
| `constitution_1973.txt` | Constitution of Pakistan, 1973 | 307 Articles | Pakistan Code |
| `qanun_e_shahadat.txt` | Qanun-e-Shahadat Order, 1984 (P.O. X) | 168 Articles | Pakistan Code |
| `peca_2016.txt` | Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act, 2016 (Act XL) | 85 | Pakistan Code |
| `mflo_1961.txt` | Muslim Family Laws Ordinance, 1961 (Ord. VIII) | 12 | Pakistan Code |
| `punjab_rent_2009.txt` | Punjab Rented Premises Act, 2009 | 4 ⚠️ **excerpt** | punjablaws.gov.pk (site returning 503) |

## Refreshing

```bash
python scripts/fetch_laws.py            # all statutes
python scripts/fetch_laws.py ppc crpc   # a subset
python scripts/check_corpus.py          # coverage report
python scripts/ingest_laws.py           # rebuild the FAISS index  <-- required
```

Laws are amended. Re-run periodically; the PDFs carry an "Updated till" date.

## Extraction notes

The official PDFs need real repair before they are usable as a retrieval corpus.
`fetch_laws.py` handles each of these, and each was a bug found by checking the
output against known law rather than assuming:

- **Contents pages.** Each PDF opens with a full table of contents that extracts
  as thousands of `Section N. <title>` lines with no provision text. Left in,
  every real section gets a near-identical twin in the vector store and retrieval
  returns the stub. Removed by keeping only the longest body per section number.
- **Repeating numbers.** The CPC's First Schedule numbers Rules *within* Orders
  (Order I r.1, Order II r.1 …), so that dedupe would delete most of the Schedule.
  CPC therefore opts out (`dedupe=False`) and keeps some contents noise instead —
  the lesser harm.
- **Split numbers.** `" 125. Votes on account"` was matched as `25.` because the
  section pattern lacked a digit guard. Deduplication then preferred that long
  body over the real Article 25 — **the Constitution's equality clause was
  silently replaced by a budget provision.** Fixed by anchoring to line-start.
- **Amendment footnotes.** Amended provisions render as `1[10A. Right to fair
  trial` and `4[489F. Dishonestly issuing a cheque`. Without handling that
  prefix, every amended section is skipped — and amendments are exactly the
  modern, most-asked-about provisions.
- **Homoglyphs.** 330 occurrences of `U+037E` (GREEK QUESTION MARK) in the PPC
  alone. It looks identical to `;` but no search for `;` will ever match it.
- **Soft hyphens.** 700+ invisible `U+00AD` in the PPC: `qatl<AD>i<AD>amd` does
  not match a search for `qatl-i-amd`.

## Verified

19/19 spot-checks against known law pass, including PPC §302 (qatl-i-amd/qisas),
§420, §489F (dishonoured cheque), §376, §337A; CrPC §154 (FIR), §497 (bail);
PECA §20/§21; MFLO §6/§7 (talaq); Constitution Art 8, 10A, 19, 25, 125, 199;
QSO Art 17.

## Known gap

`punjab_rent_2009.txt` is still a 1KB excerpt. Punjab's provincial sites
(`punjablaws.gov.pk`, `punjabcode.punjab.gov.pk`) were returning HTTP 503 at the
time of fetching. Retry later, or add the Act by hand from an official copy.
Rent questions will hit the "No statute matched" notice until then — which is
the correct, honest behaviour.

---

# Retrieval tuning (added after the corpus landed)

Filling the corpus exposed that the rest of the RAG pipeline had been tuned —
or rather, never tuned — against 21KB of excerpts. Three settings were wrong in
ways that only became visible once there was real text to retrieve. Measure with
`python scripts/eval_retrieval.py`.

## 1. The similarity threshold rejected almost everything

`SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD` is an L2 distance over **normalised** embeddings, so it
relates to cosine as `L2 = sqrt(2 - 2*cos)`. The old value of `0.85` therefore
demanded **cosine ≥ 0.64** — near-duplicate-paraphrase territory. A real question
against statute prose scores ~0.3–0.5.

Measured on the real index, `0.85` kept **2 of 10** genuine questions. *"What is
the punishment for murder?"* was rejected. **The entire corpus would have been
unreachable**, and every answer would have shown the "No statute matched" notice.

It went unnoticed because with a 21KB corpus retrieval returned nothing anyway —
the threshold was never exercised.

Now `1.05`, measured: keeps 16/17 real questions, admits 0/6 nonsense ones.

## 2. The embedding model could not read Urdu

`all-MiniLM-L6-v2` is English-only. This app has an Urdu toggle and its users
write Roman Urdu. On *"مجھے ضمانت کیسے ملے گی؟"* (how do I get bail?):

| model | correct chunk | decoy | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| all-MiniLM-L6-v2 | 0.128 | 0.134 | **chance — cannot read the script** |
| paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 | **0.513** | 0.240 | clean hit |

Now multilingual by default. Same 384 dims, larger download (~470MB).

**Thresholds are model-specific.** The first calibration gave 1.25 for the
English model; after switching, 1.25 let junk through and the correct value was
1.05. `_verify_index_integrity()` now refuses to load an index built with a
different model, so a forgotten rebuild fails loudly instead of silently
returning nonsense.

## 3. Court forms were beating the actual law

The CrPC ships Schedule V (summonses, warrants, charge templates) and the CPC
ships Appendices A–H (pleadings, decrees). They are fill-in-the-blank paperwork,
but they are dense with legal vocabulary, so they won retrieval.

*"What is the punishment for murder?"* returned a **blank charge sheet** —
*"That you, on or about the day of ___, committed murder ... punishable under
section 302"* — at L2 0.399, beating the actual PPC §302. Formally similar,
practically useless.

Now stripped (`stop_at` in `fetch_laws.py`): 62KB from the CrPC, 239KB from the
CPC. The CrPC's **Schedule II is deliberately kept** — the bailable/cognizable
table is real law people ask about.

> The guard matters: the cut uses the **last** match and only if it falls past
> the halfway mark. "APPENDIX A" also appears in the CPC's contents page at 1% —
> cutting there would have silently deleted 99% of the Code.

## 4. A missing statute, found by evaluation

*"Khula ka procedure kya hai?"* — one of the app's own homepage sample questions —
retrieved nothing useful. Khula is **not** in the Muslim Family Laws Ordinance,
which only covers talaq; it is the **Dissolution of Muslim Marriages Act 1939**,
which wasn't in the corpus. Now fetched (`dmma_1939.txt`).

It still misses on the literal Roman-Urdu word "khula", because the Act says
"dissolution of marriage" and never uses the term. It is correctly **cut by the
threshold**, so the user gets an honest "No statute matched" rather than a
confident wrong answer. Fixing it properly means query expansion
(khula → dissolution of marriage), which is a real feature, not a tweak.

## Current measured performance

```
Model      : paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2
Threshold  : 1.05
Chunks     : 3,364

recall@5   : 15/17   (what the LLM actually receives)
junk let through : 0/6
```

recall@5 is the honest metric — the retriever passes `top_k=5` chunks to the
LLM, so a correct provision at rank 3 is a success. Judging on rank 1 alone
understates the system badly (62% vs 94% on the same index).

---

# Setup order (matters)

```bash
cp .env.example .env          # set JWT_SECRET_KEY + ADMIN_EMAIL
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
python backend/scripts/seed_db.py        # 21 cases + 12 topics  <-- easy to miss
python backend/scripts/fetch_laws.py     # official statute text (already included)
python backend/scripts/ingest_laws.py    # build the FAISS index <-- required
python backend/scripts/eval_retrieval.py # verify retrieval
```

`seed_db.py` is separate from startup seeding (`main.py` only seeds lawyers).
Without it the Case Law page is a working UI over an empty table — it renders
"Nothing matched" for every query, which reads as broken. The app now logs a
warning at startup when the case table is empty.
